topic: Two friends plan separate birthday parties on the same day
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email landed in my inbox like a physical blow. "Party coordination: Sarah’s 30th, Saturday." I reread the subject line three times, my jaw tightening until my molars ached. I looked at Liam’s proposed schedule—a perfectly balanced itinerary for two separate celebrations on the same fateful day. A slow, dangerous heat began to rise in my chest. I slammed my laptop shut, the sound echoing sharply in the quiet apartment. "Are you serious?" I managed, my voice dangerously level, though I could feel the tremor in my hands. I stood up, pacing the small living room, my eyes darting around as if searching for an escape route. This felt like a deliberate slight, a profound disregard for my feelings.

[story 2]

I watched Liam meticulously color-code the invitations, a small, infuriating smile playing on his lips. It was Saturday. The day I had meticulously planned a surprise gathering for my sister, the day I had envisioned a shared afternoon with my closest friends. When he casually mentioned his plan to throw a separate event for his own birthday simultaneously, a cold, hard knot formed beneath my ribs. I gripped my coffee mug so tightly the ceramic threatened to crack. "That's just… inconsiderate, Liam," I stated, my tone clipped, every syllable precise and sharp. I took a deep, shaky breath, trying to keep the tremor out of my voice, but my knuckles were white where I held the mug. The injustice of it settled over me, heavy and suffocating.

[story 3]

My vision blurred slightly as I stared at the shared calendar. Two distinct celebrations, scheduled for the same twenty-four hours. The sheer audacity of the arrangement made my stomach clench with a violent churning. I felt a sudden, intense pressure behind my eyes. I walked over to the window, staring out at the mundane street, my breath catching in shallow gasps. When Liam approached, radiating an air of cheerful obliviousness, I didn't bother with pleasantries. "This is unacceptable," I ground out, my voice low and vibrating with suppressed intensity. I crossed my arms tightly across my chest, a rigid posture signaling that I was entirely closed off to his explanation. I waited, letting the silence stretch, daring him to justify such a thoughtless decision.
